signals.reasoning: BTC is range-bound after the flush and the board is under full geopolitical veto. There is not enough clean directional edge to justify a fresh position here.
ETH remains in the same post-flush stabilization state as BTC. With macro still RED and event risk elevated, it stays watch-only.
SOL has some residual squeeze fuel and a constructive bounce, but full veto keeps this from being a responsible fresh long. The actual chart still needs cleaner confirmation.
TST has moved sharply already and is too thin and too event-sensitive to trust here. The right call is to avoid forcing it.
JTO has strong negative-funding squeeze mechanics, but the move is already well underway and the active veto makes chasing it a poor-quality trade. It stays on the watchlist only.
GOAT is still damaged and unstable. It is too late to short cleanly and too weak to trust as a long reversal.
STBL has very crowded-long characteristics with elevated positive funding, but price is not breaking down cleanly enough to justify a fresh short under full veto. Best left alone.
0G remains a strong raw squeeze name on paper, but it is extended, volatile, and extremely headline-sensitive. Under full veto that makes it watch-only, not actionable.
AAVE has a respectable rebound and good liquidity, but it is already extended enough that reward-to-risk is poor in this macro regime. No fresh edge here.
STABLE still has very strong squeeze metrics, but the move is already mature and highly exposed to event whipsaw. It does not clear the bar for a fresh long under veto.
GRASS has bounced, but not in a way that justifies a new position while the geopolitical backdrop is still unresolved. It remains a watchlist name only.
